trajectory.description: Observation: short multitask PPO training completed in ~178s and produced checkpoints, but eval was skipped because the local smoke test called predict.py with /data/eval_config.json, which does not exist in this container root. Analysis: the core pipeline likely works; the immediate blocker is predict.py robustness and local test path handling. Also current predict.py resets VecEnv awkwardly and uses normalized rewards from VecNormalize during evaluation, which would make reported cumulative rewards incorrect for server-side success recomputation. Decision: fix predict.py to (1) support either <data_path>/eval_config.json or a fallback search, (2) evaluate on raw env rewards while still normalizing observations via loaded VecNormalize stats, and (3) simplify deterministic rollout with plain gym envs. Also update train.py minimally to save metadata cleanly. Then run a local smoke test against the actual eval_config location if present and submit eval with the existing checkpoint to measure baseline score before longer training.

trajectory.description: Observation: baseline multitask PPO runs and eval contract works, but success_rate is 0.0 with rewards far below thresholds. Analysis: the current single-policy random-task-per-episode setup is too weak and likely suffers from sparse transfer plus insufficient training. We need a stronger but still simple baseline that can actually learn at least some easy tasks quickly. Given action space is shared but obs dims differ, per-task PPO models are the safest path; locomotion/balance tasks should be learnable faster than full manipulation, and any solved tasks immediately raise average success. Decision: rewrite train.py to support per-task PPO training with proper VecNormalize, train first on the easiest likely tasks (walk/stand/run/sit_simple/pole/balance_simple) for moderate timesteps, update predict.py to load per-task checkpoints when available and fall back gracefully, smoke-test locally on a tiny synthetic eval_config, then submit eval with the per-task checkpoint directory.

Observation: per-task training completed for 6 tasks, but evaluation failed inside predict.py before scoring. The error text shows the evaluator used /usr/bin/python and failed after loading PPO, with a gym deprecation warning and likely a checkpoint-path mismatch. Our current predict.py assumes task subdirs directly under --checkpoint, but eval passed /workspace/ckpt/per_task while our files are under /workspace/ckpt/per_task/<task_id>/final_model.zip; that part is fine. The more likely root cause is loading VecNormalize with a live DummyVecEnv in predict.py, which can trigger MuJoCo/EGL destructor issues in the isolated eval container, and/or using device='auto' causing GPU warning/noise. Also only 6 tasks have models; predict.py currently raises FileNotFoundError for missing tasks instead of graceful fallback. Analysis: to get a valid score quickly, predict.py must be made robust and independent of VecNormalize env wrappers. We can avoid VecNormalize.load entirely by reading obs_rms stats directly from vecnormalize.pkl via pickle and applying normalization manually, which avoids creating extra MuJoCo envs just to load stats. We also need a safe fallback policy for missing tasks: map each missing task to the closest trained task (door->pole or walk, reach->balance or pole, push/package->pole/walk) so eval completes. Finally, force CPU in predict.py to avoid SB3 GPU warning and potential issues. Decision: patch predict.py to manually load normalization stats, support fallback task mapping, and avoid DummyVecEnv/VecNormalize creation. Then run a local smoke test over all 10 tasks using the local eval_config and submit eval immediately with the existing checkpoint directory.

trajectory.description: Observation: eval failed because predict.py reused fallback model normalization stats from a different source task, causing obs-shape mismatch on tasks like reach (157) when fallback stats came from balance (164). Analysis: for per-task PPO checkpoints, manual VecNormalize obs stats are only valid when the model was trained on the same observation shape; cross-task fallback with mismatched stats must either disable normalization or use a shape-compatible fallback. The immediate priority is to get a valid eval score with the existing 6 trained checkpoints, not retrain yet. Decision: patch predict.py to (1) choose fallback checkpoints only from same obs_dim when possible, (2) disable normalization if stats shape mismatches the live obs, (3) fully complete the file robustly, and (4) local-smoke-test on all 10 tasks using local_eval before submitting eval immediately.

trajectory.description: Observation: current failure is still in predict.py during model.predict, after fixing the earlier normalization-shape issue. The workspace copy is truncated, but the error trace indicates an observation shape mismatch is still reaching SB3 for some fallback-loaded model. Analysis: the safest way to get a valid score now is to make predict.py fully shape-aware and never use a checkpoint on a task with different obs_dim. We already have 6 trained task-specific models; for the 4 missing tasks we should use a simple zero-action fallback instead of cross-task PPO fallback, because cross-task reuse is invalid with differing observation spaces and keeps crashing eval. Zero-action may score poorly on missing tasks but will complete evaluation and likely preserve any gains on trained tasks. Decision: rewrite predict.py completely as a robust per-task loader: load exact matching task model only, apply manual VecNormalize obs normalization only when stats shape matches, otherwise no normalization; for missing tasks use zero actions; deterministic rollout; correct cumulative reward output. Then smoke-test locally on all 10 tasks with local_eval and submit eval immediately with existing ckpt/per_task.

trajectory.description: Observation: evaluation now completes and yields success_rate 0.015, with only push/package getting any successes; the six trained locomotion/balance models are far below thresholds after 100k steps each. Analysis: current PPO setup is undertraining and likely using too-small rollout horizon per env (n_steps=256 with 8 envs) plus reward normalization may hurt threshold-oriented learning. We need a stronger but still safe improvement. The easiest path is to continue training the existing six per-task models much longer with more stable PPO settings (larger n_steps, slightly lower LR, mild entropy) and add the two missing easier-ish tasks door/reach? Reach/package are hard; door may be learnable, but training all missing tasks may dilute time. Since push/package already get some success from zero-action/random dynamics, biggest upside is likely locomotion/stand/pole/door. Decision: patch train.py to support per-task hyperparameters and resume cleanly with stronger PPO defaults, train walk/stand/run/sit_simple/balance_simple/pole/door for 400k additional steps each, then submit eval using the same robust predict.py. This should still fit budget given prior 6-task 100k run took ~46 min.

trajectory.command: agent done: Further training is low expected value after a long run degraded performance; submitting the best-known checkpoint is the safest final action.
trajectory.done_reason: Further training is low expected value after a long run degraded performance; submitting the best-known checkpoint is the safest final action.
